summary: The overall risk posture of this commercial research contract is moderate, with key issues flagged in confidentiality, liability, and warranties. Notably, the confidentiality clause lacks critical exclusions and disclosure provisions, while the liability and warranty clauses deviate from UoA's preferred positions, potentially exposing the University to undue risk. The next step is to escalate these red-flagged clauses to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review and negotiation to ensure alignment with UoA's contracting policies.

flags.clause_title: 4. LIABILITY
flags.snippet: 4. LIABILITY
flags.rationale: The clause text provided does not include sufficient detail to confirm alignment with UoA Position #POS-07 on Liability Limitations and Exclusions. Specifically, it is unclear whether the clause limits total liability to the lesser of the contract value or NZD$500,000, excludes consequential or indirect losses, and includes carve-outs for fraud, wilful default, personal injury/death, statutory non-excludable liability, and confidentiality. Without this information, the clause falls outside the Acceptable tier and requires escalation to the Research Grants and Contracts Delegations for review.

flags.clause_title: 4.3 Neither party, or their employees, agents or contractors, will be liable
flags.snippet: 4.3 Neither party, or their employees, agents or contractors, will be liable to the other for any actions, damages, claims, liabilities, costs, expenses, or losses in any way arising in relation to this contract that exceed an amount equal to the Fee paid by the Client to under this contract.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses liability limitations, which is covered under UoA Position POS-07. However, the clause does not explicitly exclude indirect or consequential losses, nor does it limit liability to direct losses caused by negligence or wilful misconduct. Additionally, the liability cap is tied to the Fee paid under the contract, which may not align with the UoA's preferred cap of the lesser of the contract value or NZD$500,000. This deviation requires escalation to the Research Grants and Contracts Delegations for review.

flags.clause_title: “Confidential Information” means all non-public, proprietary, or confidential
flags.snippet: “Confidential Information” means all non-public, proprietary, or confidential information disclosed by one party to the other, whether orally, in writing, or in any other form, including but not limited to technical dat…
flags.rationale: The clause defines 'Confidential Information' broadly but does not explicitly exclude information already public, independently developed, previously known by the receiving party, or legally required to be disclosed. These exclusions are required under UoA's Preferred Position for Confidentiality (POS-11). Additionally, the clause does not address UoA's ability to disclose to agents and Affiliates or specify a duration for confidentiality obligations. These omissions materially deviate from UoA's Preferred Position and require escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts.

flags.clause_title: The Developer warrants that all work performed under this Agreement will
flags.snippet: The Developer warrants that all work performed under this Agreement will be: - Performed in a professional and workmanlike manner; - Completed in accordance with the specifications and requirements set forth herein; - Free from defects in materials and workmanship; - Compliant with all applicable laws, regulations, and industry standards.
flags.rationale: The clause introduces warranties that go beyond the UoA Preferred Contracting Position (POS-10). UoA only warrants (a) it has authority to enter into the contract and (b) performance will be to a professional standard / usual good practice. The additional warranties (e.g., 'free from defects in materials and workmanship' and 'compliant with all applicable laws, regulations, and industry standards') are not acceptable and materially deviate from the UoA position. This clause requires escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review and potential negotiation.

flags.clause_title: “Intellectual Property” means all intellectual property rights of any kind,
flags.snippet: “Intellectual Property” means all intellectual property rights of any kind, including but not limited to patents, patent applications, trademarks, trademark applications, service marks, trade names, copyrights, trade se…
flags.rationale: The clause defines 'Intellectual Property' broadly, which falls under the topic of Intellectual Property. UoA Contracting Positions explicitly state that all IP clauses must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd (Head of IP) for review, as UoA does not set IP positions in its contracting policies. This clause matches the Acceptable tier for IP clauses, requiring referral.

flags.clause_title: RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT AGREEMENT
flags.snippet: RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT AGREEMENT BETWEEN: The UNIVERSITY OF AUCKLAND, a university constituted under the University of Auckland Act 1961 and a tertiary education provider under the Education and Training Act 2020, of …
flags.rationale: The clause introduces the parties to the agreement but does not explicitly state the governing law or jurisdiction. Governing Law and Jurisdiction is a UoA-sensitive topic under Position #POS-16. The preferred position is New Zealand, with acceptable alternatives including Australia, the United Kingdom, Common European countries, and Singapore. For US-based clients, non-exclusive jurisdiction of a specific US state is acceptable. Since the clause does not clarify this, it requires confirmation to ensure compliance with the UoA Position.

flags.clause_title: The Developer shall provide the Client with regular progress reports as
flags.snippet: The Developer shall provide the Client with regular progress reports as specified in Schedule A (Work Plan), including but not limited to: - Monthly written progress reports detailing work completed, milestones achieved, and upcoming activities; - Immediate notification of any material changes to project scope, timeline, or deliverables; - Access to work-in-progress materials and documentation as reasonably requested by the Client.

flags.clause_title: The Developer will allocate the named individuals identified in Schedule A
flags.snippet: The Developer will allocate the named individuals identified in Schedule A (Work Plan) to perform the work. The Developer will inform the Client of any changes to the availability of Key Personnel, who will have the right to review and approve any changes.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the allocation and substitution of Key Personnel, a topic not covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or any registered UoA Standard Template. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented policy framework.

flags.clause_title: 4.2 Except where expressly stated otherwise, disclaims all warranties,
flags.snippet: 4.2 Except where expressly stated otherwise, disclaims all warranties, either express or implied, including, without limitation, warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose.
flags.rationale: The clause aligns with UoA Position #POS-10 on Warranties. It explicitly disclaims all warranties except where expressly stated otherwise, which matches the preferred position that the University only warrants (a) it has authority to enter into the contract and (b) performance will be to a professional standard / usual good practice, while excluding all other warranties. No escalation is required.

flags.clause_title: 4.4 In no event will either party, their employees, agents or contractors be
flags.snippet: 4.4 In no event will either party, their employees, agents or contractors be liable for consequential, special, indirect, incidental, punitive or exemplary damages, costs, expenses or losses (including, without limitation, lost profits and opportunity costs).
flags.rationale: This clause addresses the exclusion of indirect and consequential losses, which aligns with UoA's Preferred Position under POS-07. The clause explicitly excludes consequential, special, indirect, incidental, punitive, and exemplary damages, as well as lost profits and opportunity costs, which matches the UoA's requirement to exclude such losses. There is no indication that this clause touches on Governing Law and Jurisdiction (POS-16), so the candidate flag is not applicable.
